data.title: Arista Stock Surges After Q4 Beat, Q1 Sales Outlook Tops Estimates
data.description:
Arista Networks, Inc. (NYSE:ANET) shares are trading higher Friday after the company reported better-than-expected fourth-quarter financial results and issued first-quarter sales guidance above estimates on Thursday after the market closed.

  • Arista Networks shares are climbing with conviction. What’s fueling ANET momentum?

Fourth-Quarter Performance And Outlook

Arista reported adjusted earnings per share of 82 cents, beating the consensus estimate of 76 cents. In addition, the company reported revenue of $2.48 billion, beating the consensus estimate of $2.38 billion.

“Our Q4 results underscore the strong operating leverage inherent in our model, providing a powerful exit rate as we head into 2026,” said Chantelle Breithaupt, Arista’s CFO.

“By pairing 29% revenue growth with a disciplined 47.5% operating margin, Arista achieved a historic milestone: surpassing $1 billion in quarterly net income. Congratulations to the team.”

During the quarter, Arista unveiled its R4 series platforms for AI, data center and routed backbone deployments and expanded Arista AVA with additional agentic AI capabilities for multi-domain event correlation, continuous monitoring and network troubleshooting.

Looking ahead, Arista sees first-quarter revenue of $2.60 billion, versus the consensus estimate of $2.44 billion.

Consensus Ratings And Analyst Action

Arista carries a consensus rating of Buy and holds a consensus price target of $216.26. Following the earnings report, multiple analysts issued price target adjustments:

  • Needham analyst Ryan Koontz maintained a Buy rating on Arista and raised the price target from $165 to $185.
  • Keybanc analyst Brandon Nispel maintained an Overweight rating on Arista and raised the price target from $170 to $178.

Bullish Trend With Conflicting Momentum Signals

Arista Networks is currently trading 6.5% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 10.7% above its 50-day SMA, indicating strong short-term momentum. Shares have increased 23.12% over the past 12 months and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows, suggesting a robust long-term trend.

The RSI is at 48.99, which is considered neutral territory, while MACD is below its signal line, indicating bearish pressure on the stock. The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum, reflecting some uncertainty in the stock’s immediate outlook.

  • Key Support: $125.50
  • Key Resistance: $152.00

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Arista Networks, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: 9.57 — The stock is considered overvalued relative to peers.
  • Growth: 80.56 — Indicates strong growth potential.
  • Quality: 88.02 — Reflects a solid balance sheet and operational efficiency.
  • Momentum: 60.9 — Suggests moderate momentum in the stock’s price action.

The Verdict: Arista’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a company with strong growth and quality metrics, although its value score indicates it may be trading at a premium. Investors should consider the momentum score as a sign of potential price stability, but remain cautious of the high valuation relative to peers.

Arista Shares Climb Higher

ANET Price Action: At the time of publication, Arista shares are trading 7.33% higher at $145.02, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: XRP Co-Creator Calls Bitcoin A 'Technological Dead End'—Here's Why
data.description:
Ripple CTO Emeritus David Schwartz called Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) a “technological dead end” comparable to the dollar as XRP (CRYPTO: XRP) is consolidating after a V-shaped recovery from $1.15 lows.

The ‘Dead End’ Argument

Schwartz on Thursday on X responded to a user asking if he’d considered working on Bitcoin again after co-creating the XRP Ledger. 

“Not really. I think Bitcoin is largely a technological dead end for the same reason the dollar is,” he wrote.

Schwartz argued Bitcoin’s success doesn’t depend on blockchain innovation. “The technology just doesn’t seem to matter all that much to its success, at least not at the blockchain layer.”

He explained the core function investors care about isn’t new features but the simple expectation that Bitcoin remains usable and transferable in the future. 

“For 99% of what makes Bitcoin interesting, all the blockchain needs to be able to do is allow people to rely on being able to hold and transfer Bitcoin in the future,” he said.

However, he acknowledged one exception: Bitcoin will eventually need a “fork to be quantum proof.” Without quantum-resistant upgrades, “Bitcoin will collapse.”

The Centralization Debate

The comments emerged from a broader debate about XRP Ledger’s genesis. 

Bitcoin advocate Bram Kanstein claimed XRP’s “missing” early history proves centralization—the ledger starts at block 32,570, not block 1.

Schwartz countered that Bitcoin experienced “at least two incidents that showed way more centralization,” specifically referencing the coordinated 2010 rollback after the value overflow bug.

The value overflow bug created 184 billion Bitcoin in August 2010. Bitcoin’s stakeholders actively intervened to fix the critical bug, rolling back the blockchain—an example of centralized coordination that critics cite to prove Bitcoin’s immutability relies on human intervention.

On XRP’s genesis, Schwartz argued the decision was “not to make any coordinated changes and just live with it” when Ledgers 1-32,569 were lost due to a software bug in June 2012.

The Technical Setup

XRP is consolidating after recovering from the $1.15 capitulation low. 

The Supertrend at $1.2989 flipped green below price for the first time since the decline began—a bullish development suggesting near-term momentum shifted to buyers.

The price sits just below the middle Bollinger Band at $1.3782, which acts as immediate resistance. 

Immediate support is the Supertrend at $1.2989, with secondary support at $1.20-$1.25.

For bulls, XRP needs to break and hold above $1.40, then push through the upper Bollinger Band at $1.4408. 

Resistance stacks at $1.40, $1.45-$1.50, and the descending trendline from $2.5 peaks.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Here's How Much $1000 Invested In Analog Devices 10 Years Ago Would Be Worth Today
data.description:
Analog Devices (NASDAQ:ADI) has outperformed the market over the past 10 years by 6.84% on an annualized basis producing an average annual return of 20.34%. Currently, Analog Devices has a market capitalization of $161.74 billion.

Buying $1000 In ADI: If an investor had bought $1000 of ADI stock 10 years ago, it would be worth $6,284.09 today based on a price of $331.00 for ADI at the time of writing.

data.title: Expedia CFO Flags 'Dynamic' Economy, Dials Down Expectations On Margins
data.description:
The online travel giant topped Wall Street estimates on both revenue and earnings, posted double-digit booking growth, boosted its dividend, and repurchased $1.7 billion in stock, yet the stock fell nearly 7% in premarket trading as guidance signaled a more measured outlook for 2026.

Earnings Snapshot

Revenue rose 11% year over year (Y/Y) to $3.55 billion, exceeding the street view of $3.419 billion.

Adjusted EBITDA rose 32% Y/Y to $848, with EBITDA margin expanded 368 bps Y/Y to 23.9% in the fourth quarter.

Adjusted EPS surged 58% Y/Y to $3.78, surpassing the street view of $3.33.

Operating cash flow stood at $304 million, and free cash flow came in at $119 million for the quarter.

The company ended the year with unrestricted cash and short-term investments of $5.7 billion.

Key Metrics

Booked room nights rose 9% Y/Y in the quarter.

Total gross bookings also rose 11% Y/Y in the fourth quarter, led by strong execution and sustained market momentum.

B2C and B2B gross bookings increased 5% Y/Y and 24% Y/Y, respectively, in the quarter. Lodging gross bookings upped 13% Y/Y in the quarter.

The company repurchased around 9 million shares for $1.7 billion in 2025.

Expedia raised its dividend per share by 20% and declared a quarterly dividend of 48 cents per share on February 12, 2025.

Outlook

The company expects sales of $15.600 billion-$16.000 billion (versus consensus of $15.691 billion) in 2026 and $3.320 billion-$3.370 billion versus consensus of $3.225 billion) in the first quarter of FY26.

On the earnings call, Scott Schenkel, CFO, Expedia Group, said, “The upper end of our range implies stability and growth on an FX neutral basis, while the lower end of the range reflects a more cautious view, given the dynamic macro environment.” 

First-quarter margins are expected to improve on lower staffing levels and reduced marketing and cloud expenses, though the remainder of the year could be relatively muted, Schenkel added.

EXPE Price Action: Expedia Group shares were down 6.71% at $212.00 during premarket trading on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image by Bangla press via Shutterstock
